The final proof should be stated as a certificate theorem

Use the fixed orientationœÅS:=rS‚àí1.Then organize the proof as the following certificate chain.Certificate 0 ‚Äî orientation.All substitutions send œÅS‚Ü¶rH. This avoids sign ambiguity; ‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©=‚ü®‚ü®rS‚àí1‚ü©‚ü©.

R=EfŒõ‚âÖZ2 [[t]],t=s‚àí1,the raw row(s2+1,s‚àí1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to (2,t),because(s2+1)‚àí(s+1)(s‚àí1)=2.Thus the coefficient problem is governed by the regular Koszul row (2,t), not by the raw 2t+t2 term. The first t-layer is contractible; the first 2-Bockstein maps satisfy Bd‚ÜÝHd(2)for d‚â•4; and higher (2,t)-adic layers are killed by the Magnus-degree-first Koszul‚ÄìWitt cascade.Certificate F ‚Äî finite filtered Nielsen compactness.For a degree cutoff N and an open two-sided ideal J‚äÇŒõ, setPN,J:=P‚à©(1+IN+JM).

The finite solution sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data with first layer UmodJ, satisfying the finite cocycle relation for œÑœÉ=œÑ2, and sendingœÅS=rS‚àí1‚Ü¶rH (modPN,J).These finite sets are nonempty and functorial under refinement. Compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit datum, hence a continuous over-G automorphism Œ¶:F‚ÜíF with Œ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.

Final manuscript warning The only presentation point I would still police carefully is the right-row convention. In the manuscript, write the Ef-piece map as a row action:(a,b)U0=(a(s‚àí1)+b(s‚àí2),a+2b),so that (s+3,‚àí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚àí1).

Do not switch between U0 (a,b) and (a,b)U0 without explicitly declaring the convention; otherwise a harmless transpose ambiguity can make the correct H1 -move look false.

Status Inside the attached sweep, no structural mathematical gap remains. The remaining issue is external reproducibility for a standalone paper: attach the verifier scripts, matrices, or pivot tables realizing Certificates B‚ÄìD, especially D.1, D.2, and the D.3 stack-rank 60‚Üí61. The proof itself should now stay frozen in the certificate-contract form rather than reopening new obstruction layers.
